title: Troubleshooting SSO Login Failures
text:
Q: What should I do if I'm experiencing SSO login failures, authentication loops, or account lockouts?
A: If you're facing SSO login failures, follow these troubleshooting steps: Windows: Step 1: Clear your browser cache by going to Settings > Privacy and Security > Clear Browsing Data. Select 'Cached images and files' and click 'Clear Data'. macOS: Step 1: Open Safari, go to Preferences > Privacy > Manage Website Data, then remove all data. Step 2: Ensure your system time and date are correct, as discrepancies can cause authentication issues. Windows: Right-click on the time in the taskbar, select 'Adjust date/time', and ensure 'Set time automatically' is enabled. macOS: Go to System Preferences > Date & Time, and check 'Set date and time automatically'. Step 3: If you encounter an authentication loop, try using a different browser or incognito mode to rule out extension conflicts. Step 4: For account lockouts, wait 15 minutes and attempt to log in again. If the issue persists, contact IT support to unlock your account. Step 5: If you still experience session issues, log out of all applications and clear your cookies. Windows: Open Command Prompt and type 'ipconfig /flushdns'. macOS: Open Terminal and type 'sudo killall -HUP mDNSResponder'. Following these steps should help resolve your SSO login issues.
